source_quote: One is subject to liability to another for trespass, irrespective of whether he thereby causes harm to any legally protected interest of the other, if he intentionally (a) enters land in the possession of the other, or causes a thing or a third person to do so, or (b) remains on the land, or (c) fails to remove from the land a thing which he is under a duty to remove.
evidence: The DB text has the clause structure reversed with a mid-clause period 'a duty to remove. fails to remove' when the correct text structure is '(c) fails to remove from the land a thing which he is under a duty to remove.' This is a material word order error affecting the legal statement of the trespass liability test. The source PDF at lines 289-297 (Restatement Second of Torts § 158) clearly shows the correct structure.
